prompt: Create a CivicForum Operations Platform that provides an offline-first community forum backend with integrated moderation, configuration, and auditable messaging. The service supports role-based access for Administrator, Moderator, Analyst (read-only ops and reporting), and Regular User. Core resources include organization (multi-tenant clubs), forum section/subsection, thread, reply (supports nested replies up to 3 levels), tag/topic taxonomy, and thread state (pinned, featured, locked, archived). API capabilities are grouped by Authentication and Identity (username + password login only, password minimum 12 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts within 15 minutes), Forum Content (create/query/update threads and replies, apply tags, enforce state transitions such as “locked blocks new replies,” “archived blocks edits,” and “pinned limited to 3 per section”), Moderation and Back-Office Operations (ban/unban users, mute/unmute for 24 hours to 30 days, permission updates, bulk content actions, recycle bin retention 30 days with restore), and Admin Configuration (organization management, category/tag dictionaries, announcements and carousel configuration stored as ordered items with start/end timestamps, venue resources with conflict-validation rules such as preventing overlapping bookings within the same room). A messaging and notification center is included but must run fully locally: it generates in-app notification records only, triggered by events (new reply, moderation action, announcement published) and scheduled delivery (time-based jobs), honors per-user subscriptions (opt-in per category, default opt-in for security notices), tracks delivery/open timestamps, and retries failed deliveries up to 3 times with exponential backoff within 24 hours; SMS/email/WeChat and any external integrations are explicitly disabled in offline mode.The backend uses Express (TypeScript) to expose resource-level APIs and Prisma with MySQL for persistence, running as a single Docker-deployable service with no external network dependencies. Data models require tenant scoping via organizationId indexes on all user-generated entities; key constraints include unique username per organization, unique tag slug per organization, and immutable auditLogId with append-only storage. Store passwords using salted hashing, encrypt sensitive fields at rest (e.g., email if collected for display only, never for verification), and mask personal identifiers in logs. Data & Risk Audit capabilities include operation logs for login, permission changes, moderation actions, and configuration changes; dashboards return aggregated funnel metrics (visit → conversion → order → delivery) adapted for forum operations as view → registration → post → engagement, computed from event tables without third-party analytics. Abnormal behavior detection runs locally via rules (e.g., ≥10 thread deletions or ≥20 cancellations/undos within 1 hour flags a user; ≥5 reports on a thread within 30 minutes flags content for review). Support nightly local backups to an attached volume with 14-day retention and point-in-time recovery via binlog where available. Enforce API auth via session or signed tokens stored locally, rate limit per user (e.g., 120 write actions/minute, 600 reads/minute), provide structured logs, basic tracing correlation IDs, and alerting via local log thresholds only. Performance targets: p95 read under 300 ms for common queries at 500 concurrent users on a single host, with all configuration managed via feature flags stored in the database and audited.
